final_checker: stored detailed JSON verified by verify_submit_ready_artifacts.py
notes: Detailed row-by-row final Bernstein output is included as bfree_final_unified_cert_complete_results.json. The fast verifier validates the stored certificate and spot-regenerates representative rows from both checker components. The optional bfree_regenerate_final_certificate.py wrapper produces a valid current-format 180-case certificate but is not claimed to reproduce every metadata field of the packaged historical JSON byte-for-byte. The assignment rule uses centers up to 3d_1 and leaders up to a d_1; the center-only layer is reduced analytically to the layer-a envelope before invoking the 180-case certificate.
problem_scope: center-opening metric k-median: client points/leaders are legal centers; supplier variant is not claimed
runtime_claim: f(k) n^{O(1)} after Lemma scale-reduction with D_zeta=(log n)^{O(1)} poly(k,1/zeta) and the kh-adjusted FPT split
zero_distance_handling: Lemmas zero-distance-supported-edge and zero-closest-object handle averaged d_1=0 before normalization; no claim that zero-distance contraction implies d_1>0.
runtime_split_note: The enumeration exponent is O(kh) with h=Theta(k^{delta_1} log log n); the proof splits on k^{1+delta_1}(log log n)^2 <= c log n, not the smaller Anand-Lee O(k) exponent.
certificate_regeneration: bfree_regenerate_final_certificate.py regenerates a valid certificate in the current checker format; rows may not be byte-for-byte identical to the historical stored JSON metadata.
